whatItDoes: A precision stopwatch/chronometer with Start, Stop, Lap, and Reset controls. Displays time in hours:minutes:seconds.centiseconds format. Tracks laps with individual lap times, highlights the best and slowest laps, and allows copying all lap times.
whyItExists: A quick-access stopwatch is useful for timing code execution, measuring UI performance, or any task requiring precise time measurement without leaving the browser.
whoShouldUse: Developers measuring task durations, QA testers timing user flows, athletes tracking workout intervals, and anyone who needs a precise stopwatch in the browser.
useCases: Time how long a code execution or API call takes
Measure user interaction delays during testing
Track lap times for workout or activity intervals
Compare performance of different approaches by timing them
Use as a simple countdown reference
Record timing data by copying lap results
instructions: Click Start to begin the timer
Click Lap while running to record a lap time without stopping the timer
Click Stop to pause the timer at the current time
Click Reset to clear all time and lap data
The lap list shows individual lap times with BEST and SLOWEST indicators
Click Copy Laps to copy all lap times to your clipboard
examples: Start → Lap at 5s → Lap at 12s → Stop at 15s → shows 3 lap times
Use to time a for-loop execution: start before loop, stop after
Track API response times across multiple requests using laps
bestPractices: Use requestAnimationFrame-based timing (this tool's default) for sub-frame accuracy
For very precise measurements, combine with performance.now() in your code
Clear old lap data before starting a new timing session
Use the Copy Laps feature to record timing data for later analysis
The timer continues running even when the browser tab is in the background (via accumulated time)
commonMistakes: Expecting millisecond precision from setInterval — this tool uses requestAnimationFrame for better accuracy
Forgetting to reset before starting a new timing session
Confusing lap time (time since last lap) with total elapsed time
Not accounting for browser throttling when the tab is in the background
Using the stopwatch for very long durations (hours) where drift may accumulate
faq: How accurate is the timer? — It uses requestAnimationFrame which syncs with the display refresh rate (typically 60fps / ~16ms precision). For sub-millisecond timing, use performance.now() directly.
Does the timer continue when the tab is in the background? — The display may pause due to browser throttling, but accumulated time is tracked correctly when you return to the tab.
What do BEST and SLOWEST mean? — BEST highlights the fastest lap time and SLOWEST highlights the longest lap time in your current session.
Can I export lap data? — Click Copy Laps to copy all lap times to your clipboard in text format.
